word_list.word: if you lie down with dogs, you will get up with fleas
word_list.pronunciation: ɪf juː lˈaɪ dˌaʊn wɪð dˈɑːɡz juː wɪl ɡɛt ˌʌp wɪð flˈiːz

senses.definition: used to emphasize that if a person associates with people of questionable character or behavior, they are likely to adopt those negative qualities themselves

word_list.word: a goose quill is more dangerous than a lion's claw
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ ɡˈuːs kwˈɪl ɪz mˈoːɹ dˈeɪndʒɚɹəs ðˌænə lˈaɪənz klˈɔː

senses.definition: used to imply that the written word can be more influential and powerful than physical force or violence

word_list.word: evil communications (more often than not|) (tend to|) corrupt good manners
word_list.pronunciation: ˈiːvəl kəmjˌuːnɪkˈeɪʃənz mˈoːɹ ˈɔfən ðɐn nˌɑːt tˈɛnd tʊ kɚɹˈʌpt ɡˈʊd mˈænɚz

senses.definition: used to emphasize that bad company or associations can negatively influence a person's behavior and character

examples.x: Parents often warn their children that evil communications corrupt good manners, urging them to surround themselves with positive influences.

word_list.word: he (that|who) touches pitch shall be defiled
word_list.pronunciation: hiː ðæt hˌuː tˈʌtʃᵻz pˈɪtʃ ʃˌæl biː dɪfˈaɪld

senses.definition: used to warn that if someone engages in something that is considered wrong or immoral, they are likely to be negatively impacted by it, resulting in a loss of reputation or moral standing

examples.x: My grandfather used to say, he that touches pitch shall be defiled, so keep yourself away from trouble.

word_list.pronunciation: ðə ɹˈɑːʔn̩ ˈæpəl tˈuːθ ˈɪndʒɚz ɪts nˈeɪbɚz

senses.definition: used to imply that a person with bad character or behavior can negatively influence and harm the ones around them

word_list.pronunciation: ɹˈɑːʔn̩ bˈæd ˈæpəl spˈɔɪl ðə hˈoʊl bˈæɹəl bˈʊʃəl bˈʌntʃ

senses.definition: used to imply that a single bad or negative element within a group can have a harmful or corrupting influence on the entire group, leading to a decline in overall quality or integrity

examples.x: Bosses need to be wary of hiring or keeping unsuitable employees, for as the proverb states, a rotten apple spoil the barrel.

senses.definition: used to imply that associating with people who have bad habits or engage in immoral behavior can negatively impact one's own values and behavior

examples.x: The athlete decided to distance himself from teammates who engaged in doping, recognizing that bad company corrupts good morals.
